title: Remove unreachable code
description: Code that appears after control flow statements like return, break, or continue is unreachable and represents dead code. This code should be removed or the logic should be corrected to ensure proper execution flow.
recommendation: Remove unreachable statements or fix the control flow logic.

noncompliant.description: Code that appears after control flow statements like return, break, continue, or panic is unreachable and represents dead code.

compliant.description: Organizing code to ensure all statements are reachable improves maintainability and eliminates confusion about execution flow.

checks.message: Unreachable code detected after control flow statement - remove dead code or fix logic
